component.name: LinSync
component.summary: Linux-native visual file and folder comparison

description.p: LinSync is a GPL-licensed Linux application for visual file and folder comparison, merge workflows, filters, reports, and Linux-native parity.

screenshot.caption: Text and folder compare workspace with side-by-side panes
Sessions page — open tabs and recent comparison history
Filters page — glob include/exclude rules, walk options, and legacy .flt migration
Plugins page — installed comparison plugins with per-plugin enable toggle
Settings page — theme, fonts, comparison defaults, and session preferences
About page — version, license, credits, and third-party notices

description.p: Completes the previously-deferred roadmap niceties. Image compare now handles animated GIF, APNG, and WebP frame-by-frame (opt-in via an all-frames mode, with a per-frame breakdown), decodes Radiance HDR and OpenEXR sources (tone-mapped to 8-bit), and reports each side's decoded color type. OCR gains word-level positional data through an optional, backward-compatible plugin-protocol field parsed from Tesseract's TSV output. Plugins gain a per-profile enable/disable override (profile over global) wired into prediffer and archive-unpacker routing with a Plugins-page toggle, plus a conflict-resolution policy for overlapping prediffers. Image and document comparison results can now be saved as versioned JSON and re-rendered into HTML reports, matching the existing text/folder/table/binary round-trip.

Feature release completing the roadmap (Phases 4-10). Large text diffs and large folder comparisons now render lazily window-by-window so the view never loads an unbounded diff or tree at once, backed by paged core APIs and bridge endpoints. Adds rendered document compare (pdftoppm) with page-range selection, webpage rendered/screenshot compare via an out-of-process Qt WebEngine renderer with a filterable resource tree, and archive comparison through unpacker/virtualizer plugins with nested-archive recursion. The plugin layer gains option-schema validation, install/remove across core/CLI/GUI, a first-run trust prompt, per-profile prediffer routing and chaining, and sandbox-confinement reporting. Three-way merge can launch interactively as a Git mergetool; reports gain versioned JSON save/restore, relative-path portable bundles, and preview-before-export; sessions and projects persist multiple tabs with per-entry profiles and CLI commands. Accessibility adds screen-reader status/error announcements and high-contrast diff change bars, and the localization runtime is wired with a lupdate/lrelease string-catalog pipeline.

Correctness, security, and robustness hardening release. Fixes folder delete operations (which previously always failed); makes three-way merge resolve from structured conflict regions instead of re-parsing marker text (content that looks like a conflict marker no longer corrupts the result), preserve CRLF/CR line endings on save, and write atomically. The plugin sandbox now grants helpers read-only access to $HOME, applies file-descriptor and process limits on the bubblewrap fallback, and blocks the full set of credential-changing syscalls. Image compare bounds decoder memory and dimensions to defeat decompression bombs and fixes an overlay-buffer integer overflow. Settings and sessions preserve unknown JSON keys across load and save, OCR language selection now reaches the extractor plugin, and there are further fixes across table, filter, trash, webpage, the CLI, and the GUI bridge.

Renamed the application id from io.visorcraft.LinSync to com.visorcraft.LinSync to match the visorcraft.com domain. This updates the desktop entry, AppStream component id, MIME registration, icons, D-Bus and Wayland app id, and plugin ids; it also fixes stray visorcraft.io references (the security contact email and the Qt organization domain).

Security hardening across the plugin sandbox and bundled helpers: the sandbox fails closed when neither Landlock nor bubblewrap is available and clears the helper environment down to a safe allowlist, streaming helpers run under the same confinement, the web-fetch plugin pins the validated address at connect time to defeat DNS rebinding, folder copy and delete operations refuse symlinked paths that escape the comparison root, and the GUI bridge token file is written owner-only. The About page now shows the real LinSync application icon and gives the "Visit LinSync" button the same styling as the Credits and Licenses buttons.

Webpage compare is now fully functional: the plugin sandbox grants network plugins read access to the resolver configuration and TLS trust store (fixing getaddrinfo EAI_AGAIN inside the sandbox), the web-fetch plugin presents a mainstream browser User-Agent so CDN/WAF filters no longer return 403, and results render as a side-by-side editor diff with cursor, multi-row selection, keyboard navigation, line-number gutters, synchronized scrolling, and a draggable diff-overview ruler. The Plugins page now renders built-in (always-on) toggles with a themed track instead of black.

The Image, Webpage, and Document compare pages are now fully theme-aware: Kirigami.Theme propagates correctly and the shared App* form controls render with themed frames instead of stray dark borders. Image Compare gains Left/Right file pickers and Tolerance/ΔE spin boxes that match the Settings styling. The Webpage compare layout was redesigned and the Document compare page was restyled to match the other compare pages. Adds a shared AppButton control and removes the sidebar hover flash.

QML diff-pane simplification reduces per-row QML nodes 3×. Binding-loop-free theme model across 7 pages. Compare profiles are now fully wired through the HTTP bridge with 7 built-in profiles, CLI subcommand, and cxx-qt fixup landed. New reusable App* QML controls and DesignTokens theming system.

Security release. The bundled web-fetch plugin now validates outbound URLs against an SSRF policy: only http/https schemes are honoured, the resolved host must be a publicly-routable address, and redirect targets are revalidated against the same policy.

LinSync 1.1.0 adds three new compare modes: image (pure-Rust pixel + CIEDE2000 perceptual diff), document (PDF/DOCX/ODT via Tesseract OCR + Poppler + LibreOffice helpers), and webpage (rendered DOM diff via Qt WebEngine, feature-gated). New linsync-sandbox crate wires Landlock + seccompiler with a bubblewrap fallback for plugin helpers. Plugin host now propagates process groups to prevent grandchild leaks.

GUI wiring complete for Settings, Filters, and Plugins pages; three-pane merge view with save-to-third-target; linsync-cli mergetool integration. Filter grammar adds the de: / e: expression families and a migrator for legacy .flt files. Plugin protocol gains unpack_folder and streaming output, with bundled ZIP and tar unpackers. Text compare gains opt-in moved-block detection. Accessibility audit with 11 P0 fixes; per-section GUI screenshots in CI.
LinSync 1.0.0 with Rust core comparison engines, CLI workflows, and the QML/Kirigami shell.
